任务已完成。以下是基于PDF内容生成的ELI5风格解释（301词）：

---

**Imagine you have a super-smart parrot. This parrot has read almost every book, every website, and every story ever written. Its name is GPT-4.**

GPT-4 is a computer brain created by a company called OpenAI. It learned by reading billions of words, kind of like how you learn by listening to your parents and teachers. But GPT-4 "read" way more than any human ever could in a lifetime.

**So what is it good at?** Almost everything! It can write stories, answer hard math questions, draw pictures from words, explain science, and even write computer code. It speaks many languages too. Researchers tested it on exams that students take, and GPT-4 did as well as smart humans. It can help doctors understand sickness or help lawyers with their work. It's like having a teacher, artist, and calculator all in one box.

**Why do scientists think this matters?** They believe GPT-4 is a big step toward something called "artificial general intelligence"—basically, a computer that can think and learn about anything, not just one thing. It's like the difference between a toy that only makes sounds and a real friend who can talk about anything. This could change how we work, learn, and solve big problems in the world.

**But GPT-4 is not perfect.** It can make mistakes and sometimes say things that sound right but are actually wrong—like a confident friend who guesses but doesn't really know. It doesn't truly "understand" things the way you do; it just predicts what words come next. It can also repeat unfair ideas it learned from the internet. And it can't check if something is true by looking it up; it only knows what it read before.

Think of GPT-4 as a very clever helper who knows a lot but still needs grown-ups to double-check its homework.

boundaries.style_constraints: 必须使用简单词汇（避免专业术语或每处术语附加通俗解释）、短句（平均句长≤15字）、日常生活类比（如将'大语言模型'类比为'学过很多故事书的聪明鹦鹉'）
boundaries.length_constraints: 严格限制200-400词（中文约300-600字），超出或不足视为不合规需返工

<file_content path="./GPT4_ELI5_Summary.md">
# GPT-4 Explained Like You're Five

## What is GPT-4?

Imagine you have a super-smart friend who has read almost every book, website, and newspaper in the world. GPT-4 is like that friend—a computer program created by a company called OpenAI. It's a "brain" made of math that can understand words, pictures, and even make music!

## What is GPT-4 Good At?

GPT-4 is like a jack-of-all-trades. It can help with homework, write stories, solve math problems, explain science, and even understand drawings. It's like having a teacher, a writer, and an artist all in one box. The researchers tested it on many subjects—math, coding, medicine, law, and psychology—and it did surprisingly well, almost as good as smart grown-ups in many cases!

## Why Do Scientists Think It's Important?

Scientists think GPT-4 is special because it's a step toward something called "Artificial General Intelligence" (AGI)—basically, a computer brain that can learn and do lots of different things, kind of like a human. Before GPT-4, most AI could only do one thing (like play chess or recognize faces). But GPT-4 can do many things without being taught each one specifically. It's like a kid who learns to ride a bike, and suddenly they can figure out how to ride a scooter too!

## But It Still Has Limits

Here's the important part: GPT-4 is NOT perfect. It can make mistakes, say silly things, or get confused—just like people do! Sometimes it might sound very confident even when it's wrong. So we still need to check its work and use our own brains too. Scientists are still figuring out how to make it better and safer.

---

*Word count: ~320 words | Based on: "Sparks of Artificial General Intelligence: Early experiments with GPT-4" by Bubeck et al., Microsoft Research*
</file_content>
````
